events.description: Parking for the 2026 CrossFit Games Attending Multiple Days? The 3-Day Parking Pass is the recommended option for guests attending more than one day of the CrossFit Games. Advance purchase is strongly recommended. Purchase All 3 Days Vip Parking Package (includes In and Out Privileges in the A-B-C Main Lot) Attending a Single Day? Single-day parking is available in the Delmas Lots. Single-Day Parking Pass (Delmas Lots) Valid only on the selected event day Parking access from 8 AM to 7 PM No in-and-out privileges Re-entry requires a new parking purchase Pricing Advance Purchase: $45 + applicable fees Event-Day Rate: $50 Purchase General Event Parking for Friday, 7/24 Purchase General Event Parking for Saturday, 7/25 Purchase General Event Parking for Sunday 7/26 Why Purchase Parking in Advance? Parking inventory for the CrossFit Games weekend is limited. Purchasing parking before arriving is the easiest way to ensure a smooth and stress-free event experience. Benefits of purchasing in advance: Secure your parking before lots reach capacity Avoid searching for available parking on event day Speed up your arrival experience Guarantee access to your selected parking option Guests attending multiple days are encouraged to purchase the 3-Day Parking Pass early, as ABC Lot inventory is limited.

events.title: Middle School Bike Skills
events.address: Cupertino Senior Center, 21251 Stevens Creek Blvd, 95014
events.description: Middle School Bike Skills workshops prepare rising 6th, 7th, and 8th graders to bike to middle school and beyond. The original course teaches students and their parent or guardian safe biking fundamentals. New this year is a sequel which teaches students how to adapt those skills to stay safe while riding an e-bike and a third course teaching students the basics of maintaining and doing simple repairs on their own bikes. Msbs 101 - Middle School Bike Workshop Msbs 102 - e-Bike Workshop Msbs 103 - Bike Maintenance and Repair Workshop This Middle School Bike Skills program is brought to you by Cupertino Safe Routes to School in partnership with Wheel Kids. Registration is required. Links and more information can be found at www.cupertino.gov/saferoutes/msbs
events.blurb: You and your middle schooler learn safe biking fundamentals to ride to school confidently.

events.description: Middle School Bike Skills workshops prepare rising 6th, 7th, and 8th graders to bike to middle school and beyond. events.title: The Employee Dharma Handbook
events.description: As an important rocket launch approaches, Val, an HR Executive at an aerospace company in Silicon Valley, suspects that a young engineer was overlooked for a promotion. Leela, a first generation Indian-American, doesn't really want to talk about it, but Val suspects sexism is the cause. What they don't expect to uncover are simmering tensions of ancient origins among the company's Indian immigrant employees…sorry, Valued Team Members. Soon Val is navigating a funhouse of identity, nepotism, and desire as she tries to understand the culture and biases of Leela's managers. When push comes to shove, Val will need to decide what her duty is to Leela, to the company, and to herself.
events.blurb: Watch a play about an HR executive navigating workplace bias at an aerospace company during a pivotal rocket launch.

events.title: Pooch Plunge
events.address: Blackberry Farm Park, 21979 San Fernando Avenue, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: Let your pup take the plunge during a dog-only swim in the Blackberry Farm pools. It's a paws-itively fun way for your pooch to enjoy the pools before they open for summer swimming—tail wags are highly encouraged. Swim Sessions (45 minutes of tail-wagging fun) Block 1 - 9 to 9:45 a.m. Block 2 - 10 to 10:45 a.m. Block 3 - 11 to 11:45 a.m. Block 4 - Noon to 12:45 p.m. Register Registration opens in February for the spring event and in June for the fall event. For additional information, visit the Pooch Plunge webpage.
events.blurb: Your dog swims solo in the pool during a dog-only splash session before summer opens.

events.title: Dracula
events.venue: Mountain View Center for the Performing Arts
events.description: Sink your teeth into the ultimate Halloween thrill. Steven Dietz's adaptation of the timeless classic seduces with elegance and suspense, turning the legendary vampire tale into a high-octane theatrical ride. Bold, chilling, and wickedly smart, the story draws you into a world where reason clashes with superstition and love becomes dangerously intoxicating. This Dracula keeps you leaning forward—never quite sure who holds the power, or what darkness waits just out of sight.
events.blurb: Witness the gothic horror classic rendered as a compelling stage production with talented actors.

events.venue: Mountain View Center for the Performing Arts
events.description: Sink your teeth into the ultimate Halloween thrill. Steven Dietz's adaptation of the timeless classic seduces with elegance and suspense, turning the legendary vampire tale into a high-octane theatrical ride. Bold, chilling, and wickedly smart, the story draws you into a world where reason clashes with superstition and love becomes dangerously intoxicating. This Dracula keeps you leaning forward—never quite sure who holds the power, or what darkness waits just out of sight.
events.blurb: Witness the gothic horror classic rendered as a compelling stage production with talented actors.

events.title: Monster Mash
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: Get ready for a fang-tastic good time at Cupertino Parks and Recreation's annual Monster Mash Halloween event at the Quinlan Community Center. Put on your favorite costume and enjoy an evening of games, trick-or-treating, spooky crafts, and pumpkin decorating. It's a fun-filled night of Halloween activities for the whole family. Register Registration for Monster Mash begins in late July.
events.blurb: Wear your costume and enjoy games, trick-or-treating, spooky crafts, and pumpkin decorating.

events.title: Join-A-Ranger: Wildlife Tracking
events.description: Join a Park Ranger for a fun-filled wildlife tracking class. You'll learn about species identification through their tracks and sign-tracking. Become a nature detective and learn to read the ground like a storybook through the art of tracking. Please wear sturdy walking shoes and long pants that can get dirty. All attendees must register and children must be accompanied by an adult.
events.blurb: Learn to identify animal tracks and read the ground like a nature detective with a park ranger.

events.title: Don Giovanni
events.description: DON Giovanni Back after 12 years, Opera San Jose builds a new production of Mozart's dark psychological thriller. Seduction, rebellion, and supernatural justice collide in a thrilling look at a notorious libertine's downfall. The production follows a charismatic anti-hero who pursues pleasure without remorse, leaving a trail of broken hearts and vengeful spirits in a […]
events.blurb: Watch Mozart's dark psychological thriller about a libertine's seduction, rebellion, and supernatural downfall.

events.title: Na Leo Mokupuni in Cupertino
events.description: Na Leo Mokupuni in Cupertino – meaning island sounds in Cupertino Experience the rhythms, songs, and dances of the islands! Na Leo Mokupuni brings together performers to celebrate the vibrant traditions of island music and dance—right here in Cupertino. Preregistration is recommended. Limited tickets available at the door, while supplies last.
events.blurb: Experience island rhythms, songs, and dances rooted in Hawaiian and Pacific island traditions.

events.title: Cupertino Campout
events.address: Creekside Park, 10455 Miller Avenue, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: The Cupertino Campout is a weekend to remember! Families are invited to pitch a tent at Creekside Park and join us for a night under the stars, filled with recreational activities. This campout is a fantastic way to gather with family, neighbors, and friends or make new ones, all while enjoying all kinds of fun. Pre-registration is required.
events.blurb: Pitch a tent and spend the night under the stars with recreational activities and neighbors.

events.title: Join-A-Ranger: Wild Flowers
events.description: Join a Park Ranger as you explore the native wildflowers in our local riparian habitat. We'll explore how to identify different flowers as well as the local history of native plants. All attendees must register and an adult must accompany children. The hike is approximately one-quarter mile on a combination of dirt trails and paved paths.
events.blurb: See native wildflowers with a park ranger and learn their identification and local history.

events.title: Breakfast with Santa
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, 95014
events.description: You and your family are invited to a festive breakfast celebration with Santa! Enjoy delicious North Pole pancakes prepared by Santa's elves, take photos with Santa, and explore a holiday workshop filled with crafts and activities for children. Three seatings: 8:30 a.m., 9:30 a.m., 10:30 a.m. Register Registration opens in late July.
events.blurb: Enjoy pancakes with Santa, take photos, and do holiday crafts with your family.

events.title: 2026 Cupertino Community Service Awards
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 N. Stelling Road, 95014
events.description: The third annual Cupertino Community Service Awards will take place on Wednesday, October 28. This ceremony will recognize the 2026 Crest Awards (Cupertino Recognizes Extra Steps Taken), and Commissioners who served on a City Commission. More details and registration information for the October 28 event will be available soon.
events.blurb: Watch the Cupertino Community Service Awards ceremony honoring local volunteers and commissioners.

events.title: Environmental Education Center Open House
events.description: Drop by the Environmental Education Center (EEC) at Mc Clellan Ranch Preserve every Saturday from 11 a.m. to 3 p.m. for family fun and discovery. Meet the City's animal ambassadors, explore local wildlife, engage with preserve staff and park rangers, and enjoy hands-on activities that bring nature to life.
events.blurb: Meet animal ambassadors, learn about local wildlife, and do hands-on activities with park rangers.

events.title: Signing Santa
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, 95014
events.description: Deaf and hard of hearing children, along with their families, are invited to spend time with a Santa who understands sign language! Santa only communicates using sign language but an interpreter will be on-site to help translate for friends and family. Register Registration opens in late July.
events.blurb: Meet a signing Santa who communicates entirely in ASL with your deaf or hard-of-hearing children.

events.title: Free Shakespeare in the Park Event
events.description: Free Shakespeare in the Park brings you Anthony and Cleopatra directed by Katja Rivera. Set against the backdrop of the Roman Empire and Egypt, For show details, Anthony and Cleopatra examines the conflict between duty and desire, and the impact of personal relationships. Visit sfshakes.org.
events.blurb: Watch Shakespeare's Anthony and Cleopatra performed outdoors in the amphitheater.

events.title: Tree Lighting
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: Kick off the holiday season at the City's annual Community Tree Lighting! Enjoy the moment the tree is illuminated with hundreds of twinkling lights, live music from a local choir, and festive holiday cheer. After the tree lighting, families can visit Santa and take photos.
events.blurb: Watch the holiday tree light up, hear a local choir sing, and visit Santa for photos.

events.title: Summer BBQ Social
events.address: Rengstorff Park BBQ Area, Mountain View, CA
events.description: Mid-summer celebration featuring hot dogs, fruit, chips, and a sweet treat. Adults 55+ welcome; younger guests must be accompanied by an adult 55+. Pre-registration required; tickets $5 per person (cash only) sold at Mountain View Senior Center Front Desk June 1–26.
events.blurb: Eat hot dogs, fruit, chips, and dessert at a mid-summer gathering for adults 55-plus.

events.title: Life in Los Gatos Art Camp - Week 3
events.address: New Museum Los Gatos, 106 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA
events.description: Week-long art camp for ages 8-12 where young artists create artwork capturing their perspective on Los Gatos history and charm. Participants learn art techniques, explore museum exhibitions, walk the Los Gatos Creek trail, and display their work at a mini-art show.
events.blurb: Create artwork about Los Gatos history, tour museum exhibitions, walk the creek trail, and display your work.

events.title: Baer Blacksmith Shop Demonstrations
events.description: Community members are invited to visit the Baer Blacksmith Shop from 11 a.m. to 3 p.m. and learn about early Cupertino history, the craft of blacksmithing, and learn from our experienced blacksmith, who will be firing the forge to give demonstrations.
events.blurb: Watch a blacksmith fire the forge and demonstrate the craft while learning early Cupertino history.

events.title: Friday Night at the Art Center
events.address: Palo Alto Art Center, 1313 Newell Road, Palo Alto, California 94303
events.description: Opening party for the ceramic exhibition featuring a participatory performance with artist Elana Mann, hands-on art activities, drinks, and community celebration. Members get early access and complimentary drink tickets starting at 5 p.m.
events.blurb: Attend the ceramic exhibition opening with live performance, hands-on activities, and drinks with the artist.

events.title: Wildfire preparedness workshop
events.description: Community workshop on wildfire preparedness covering home hardening, defensible space, evacuation planning, and emergency supplies. Includes resource fair with CAL Fire, American Red Cross, PG&E, and local law enforcement representatives.
events.blurb: Get wildfire safety tips and preparedness strategies from Santa Clara County Fire Department staff.

events.title: Pria Anand with Shaili Jain and Tanya Maria Luhrmann
events.description: In the award-winning book The Mind Electric, Pria Anand, a neurologist described by the Times Literary Supplement as "Oliver Sacks' most obvious heir," reckons with the stories we tell about our brains–and the stories our brains tell us.
events.blurb: Hear neurologist Pria Anand discuss how brains shape our stories and ourselves.

events.title: Coffee Companion 5th Anniversary Adoption Event
events.address: San José Animal Care Center, 2750 Monterey Road, San José, CA 95111
events.description: Free community adoption event with waived adoption fees for all shelter pets, complimentary specialty coffee and drinks, and opportunities to meet adoptable pets and learn about animal welfare and training programs from local advocates.
events.blurb: Meet adoptable shelter pets, get waived adoption fees, and taste specialty coffee.

events.title: Summer Concert Series
events.address: Memorial Park Amphitheater, 21163 Anton Way, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: Spend your Thursday evenings outdoors with free live music at the Memorial Park Amphitheater. Bring a blanket or low-back chair, settle in on the lawn, and enjoy a lineup of bands spanning classic rock, Latin rock, blues, R&B, and more.
events.blurb: Bring a blanket and catch live music spanning classic rock, Latin, blues, and R&B on the lawn.

events.title: 2026 Prospect Scrimmage
events.address: Tech CU Arena, 525 W Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113
events.description: Watch top Sharks prospects and 2026 NHL Draft picks compete in a scrimmage during Development Camp. Tickets start at $13.30, with net proceeds benefiting the Sharks Foundation. Pre-game live music and rally towel giveaway included.
events.blurb: Watch top Sharks prospects and 2026 NHL Draft picks compete in a scrimmage with live pre-game music.

events.title: 2026 Sharks Draft Viewing Party
events.address: SAP Center, 525 W Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113
events.description: Public draft viewing party at SAP Center featuring the draft broadcast on center-hung video board, exclusive merchandise, games, family activities on the arena floor, food and drink for purchase, and fan photo opportunities.
events.blurb: Watch the Sharks draft on the arena's video board, play games, grab food, and snap photos with the team.

events.title: Acga Clay & Glass Festival
events.address: Palo Alto Art Center, 1313 Newell Road, Palo Alto, California 94303
events.description: Annual festival showcasing over 130 California clay and glass artists. Features live artist demonstrations, pottery throwdown competition, hands-on clay activities, food trucks, valet parking, and family fun. Free admission.

events.title: Pal Summer Camps: July 13-17
events.address: Pacific Art League, 668 Ramona Street, Palo Alto, CA 94301
events.description: Art camps for ages 7-16 including Sewing Cozy Critters, Plein Air Drawing & Watercolor, World Builder Studio, and Birdhouse Design & Construction. Professional instructors, small class sizes, high-quality materials included.

events.title: Pal Summer Camps: June 22-26
events.address: Pacific Art League, 668 Ramona Street, Palo Alto, CA 94301
events.description: Art camps for ages 7-16 including Book Making Lab, Watercolor Basics+Beyond, Intro to Oil Painting, Sketching Around Town, and K-Pop Studio Art. Professional instructors, small class sizes, high-quality materials included.
events.blurb: Take art classes in book making, watercolor, oil painting, and K-pop studio techniques.

events.title: Brick Blast LEGO Fan Convention
events.address: 5001 Great America Parkway, Santa Clara, CA, 95054
events.description: LEGO fans unite! The Brick Team is bringing a new LEGO fan convention to the Bay Area, featuring creations inspired by space and science fiction to fit the theme "Out of This World." To purchase tickets, click view event.
events.blurb: Browse LEGO creations inspired by space and science fiction at the fan convention.

events.title: 1-on-1 Smartphone Help
events.address: Technology Center, Central Park Library, 2635 Homestead Road, Santa Clara, CA
events.description: Get personalized 1-on-1 help from Technology Consultants with iPhone or Android smartphones. Topics include photo editing, cloud storage setup, app configuration, and phone security. Book a 15-minute appointment.
events.blurb: Get personalized help from a tech consultant on smartphone features, apps, and security settings.

events.title: 4th of July Community Celebration
events.address: Mission College, 3000 Mission College Blvd, Santa Clara, CA
events.description: Free family-friendly celebration with food trucks, family activities, and live entertainment featuring headliner Pride and Joy. Bring blankets and lawn chairs for a picnic, and enjoy a spectacular fireworks show.

events.title: Make Music San José
events.description: Free music festival featuring approximately 140 artists performing in 40 participating venues across San José. All genres of music celebrating the summer solstice as part of the international Fête de la Musique.

events.title: Together in Pride
events.venue: Civic Center Plaza and Pioneer Park
events.address: Civic Center Plaza and Pioneer Park, Mountain View, CA

events.blurb: Watch live performances and visit community booths celebrating LGBTQ+ Pride.

events.title: Introduction to Zentangle Workshop
events.address: Sunnyvale Public Library, Library Program Room
events.description: Introduction to meditative drawing with Zentangle instructor Abby O'Connell. The method teaches relaxing and fun ways to create beautiful images by drawing structured patterns. No art experience necessary.

events.title: Lunar Octet Jazz Performance
events.address: Sunnyvale Public Library, Library Program Room
events.description: Award-winning Lunar Octet brings eclectic music enriched by Cuban and Puerto Rican salsa, rock, African high-life and Brazilian samba traditions. Exciting, dynamic and powerful performances for all ages.

events.description: Free community soccer festival with youth and adult 3v3 divisions, cultural food vendors, live entertainment, and lowrider showcase.
events.blurb: Watch youth and adult teams play 3v3 soccer, sample cultural food, and see lowriders.

events.title: Mayor's Office Hours at the Saratoga Farmers' Market
events.description: The Mayor invites you to join Office Hours at the Saratoga Farmers' Market the first Saturday of every month, from 10:00 AM to Noon.
events.blurb: Meet the mayor at the farmers' market to discuss community concerns and ideas.

events.title: Make Music Day San Jose
events.description: Free music celebration featuring local musicians performing live across downtown San Jose at multiple venues. No tickets required.
events.blurb: Hear live music from local musicians performing free across downtown San Jose venues.

events.title: Monthly Craft: Paper Tents
events.address: New Museum Los Gatos, 106 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA
events.description: Drop-in, self-led craft activity free with museum admission. Visitors of all ages create paper tents during regular museum hours.
events.blurb: Fold and decorate your own paper tent during museum hours.

events.title: Community & Climate: The Problem with Plastic
events.description: Author talk with Susan Keefe about plastic pollution, part of the Community & Climate lecture series on global climate action.
events.blurb: Hear author Susan Keefe discuss plastic pollution and climate action.

events.title: Movie screening: Relay
events.address: Mountain View Senior Center, Multipurpose Room B, 266 Escuela Ave, Mountain View, CA 94040
events.description: Thriller/Action film starring Riz Ahmed and Lily James about a broker who breaks his own rules to protect a client. Rated R.
events.blurb: Watch Riz Ahmed and Lily James in a high-stakes thriller about rule-breaking.

events.title: Council Neighborhoods Committee Community Meeting
events.address: Mountain View Community Center, 201 S. Rengstorff Ave., Mountain View, CA
events.description: Public neighborhood meeting to ask city staff questions about community projects and programs. Light refreshments provided.
events.blurb: Ask city staff questions about neighborhood projects and programs over refreshments.

events.title: American Cancer Society Relay for Life
events.address: Campbell Community Center Track, Campbell, CA
events.description: 24-hour community relay event benefiting the American Cancer Society. Join the Chamber team, make a donation, or volunteer.

events.title: Capitol Dance Company: Beauty & the Beast and Rococo
events.address: Heritage Theatre, 1 W. Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008
events.description: Full day of dance performances featuring two productions: Beauty & the Beast at 10:30 AM and 2:30 PM, Rococo at 7:00 PM.
events.blurb: Watch Capitol Dance Company perform Beauty & the Beast and Rococo throughout the day.

events.title: Well-being Walk
events.description: Join Peer Health Educators for a campus walk using the SJSU interactive Map! All walks begin in the SWC Wellness Lounge.
events.blurb: Walk the SJSU campus with peer educators starting from the Wellness Lounge.

events.title: California Sports Center: From Page to Stage
events.address: Heritage Theatre, 1 W. Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008
events.description: Dance recital showcasing preschool, recreational, and competitive dancers performing what they learned over the year.
events.blurb: Watch preschool, recreational, and competitive dancers perform their year-long routines at Heritage Theatre.

events.title: Make Your Own Buttons
events.address: Mountain View Public Library, 585 Franklin St., Mountain View, CA 94041
events.description: Pride-themed craft activity. Design your own button using templates or choose premade designs. All supplies provided.
events.blurb: Design your own pride button using templates or pick a premade design with all supplies included.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Match 60: Paraguay vs. Australia
events.description: FIFA World Cup 2026 Group D match between Paraguay and Australia, one of six World Cup matches hosted in Santa Clara.
events.blurb: Watch Paraguay and Australia compete in a FIFA World Cup Group D match.

events.title: Red, White and Blue Fruit Skewers
events.description: Celebrate Independence Day by making festive red, white and blue fruit skewers. Ages 3 and up. Registration required.
events.blurb: Assemble red, white and blue fruit skewers to celebrate Independence Day.

events.title: Community & Climate: Dinosaurs, Climate Change, and Resilience
events.description: Author talk with Evan Jevnikar about climate change and resilience, part of the Community & Climate lecture series.
events.blurb: Hear author Evan Jevnikar discuss climate resilience and what dinosaurs teach us.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Match 31: Turkey vs. Paraguay
events.description: FIFA World Cup 2026 Group D match between Turkey and Paraguay, one of six World Cup matches hosted in Santa Clara.
events.blurb: Watch Turkey and Paraguay compete in a FIFA World Cup Group D match.

events.title: Summer Luncheons
events.address: Montalvo Arts Center, 15400 Montalvo Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070
events.description: Al fresco lunch on the veranda with live piano music during summer afternoons. Held Wednesdays in July and August.
events.blurb: Eat lunch on the veranda with live piano music during summer afternoons on Wednesdays.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Match 44: Jordan vs. Algeria
events.description: FIFA World Cup 2026 Group J match between Jordan and Algeria, one of six World Cup matches hosted in Santa Clara.
events.blurb: Watch Jordan and Algeria compete in a FIFA World Cup Group J match.

events.title: Sunnyvale's July 4th Celebration
events.description: Celebrate America's 250th anniversary with live music, a Ferris wheel, amusement rides and more on Murphy Avenue.
events.blurb: Hear live music, ride the Ferris wheel and amusement rides, and celebrate America's 250th anniversary.

events.title: Campbell Relay for Life 2026
events.description: Largest and most inspiring cancer fundraising event supporting research, patient services, and support programs.

events.title: 2026 Campbell Summer Concert Series
events.description: Eight-concert season featuring a blend of old favorites and acts new to the Campbell Summer Concert Series.
events.blurb: Catch eight concerts featuring returning favorites and new acts downtown all summer.

events.title: Disney's Newsies
events.description: High-energy musical about scrappy New York newsboys with energetic dancing and powerful vocal performances.
events.blurb: Watch energetic dancing and powerful vocals in this high-energy musical about New York newsboys.

events.title: Toddler Time with JAMaROO (bilingual Mandarin/Cantonese and English)
events.description: Music performance for ages 2 to 5. Sing and dance with a special music performer. Drop-in; space limited.

events.title: Toddler Time with Farmer Arann
events.description: Music performance for ages 2 to 5. Sing and dance with a special music performer. Drop-in; space limited.
events.blurb: Sing and dance with a special music performer alongside other young kids.

events.description: Music performance for ages 2 to 5. Sing and dance with a special music performer. Drop-in; space limited.

events.description: Music performance for ages 2 to 5. Sing and dance with a special music performer. Drop-in; space limited.

events.title: Laser Dark Side of the Moon

events.title: San Jose Sharks draft viewing party

events.title: ESL Book Club

events.blurb: Meet visiting glass artist Jen Blazina and see her work with the community.

events.title: World Cup Art Night
events.description: Celebrate the World Cup by designing your own FIFA-inspired pennant to cheer on your favorite team.

events.title: Power Networking Lunch
